summary: Compacted PROJECT_STATUS from a 63 KB mixed current/history file into a 63-line current R0 snapshot and an independent 414-line historical ledger. The complete pre-compaction content is preserved exactly behind an archive banner; roadmap and acceptance-matrix references now point to the independent ledger.

tests_run.command: node exact ledger preservation assertion against d058cb88:PROJECT_STATUS.md
tests_run.result: After removing the added archive banner and restoring the original title, the ledger exactly equals d058cb88:PROJECT_STATUS.md. Original blob 77d8f6189dcc940009c5b1d24c98bdab3abb6e3a; archived ledger blob 939d03491983be27b02de2f9668a787f6cadac65.

tests_run.command: node current and historical status byte/line count
tests_run.result: Current PROJECT_STATUS.md is 3978 bytes / 63 lines; historical ledger is 64028 bytes / 414 lines.

tests_run.command: rg roadmap and current-status references plus old snapshot-heading exclusion
tests_run.result: PROJECT_STATUS and both roadmap matrices reference project/history/PROJECT_STATUS-M0-M7-LEDGER.md; no old numbered M3-M7 snapshot sections remain in current status.
Retained M7 worktree still has exactly 20 pre-existing status entries.

decisions.decision: Use project/history/PROJECT_STATUS-M0-M7-LEDGER.md as the non-current historical archive required by docs/29, while keeping PROJECT_STATUS.md as the sole concise current snapshot.
Preserve the complete pre-compaction source verbatim beneath an archive banner rather than summarizing or deleting historical PR, commit, Gate, Human Gate, and product-rejection evidence.

risks.summary: The independent ledger intentionally contains stale historical 'current/next' wording; its archive banner and current-status link prevent it from being treated as operational state.

requested_action: Independently verify exact historical preservation, concise current-state accuracy, reference synchronization, task scope reconciliation, and absence of product/R1 changes; ACCEPT to lead or REQUEST_CHANGES with exact evidence.
